steps.title: Find the key contrast
steps.content: Text 1 relies on non-boreal research showing warming benefits invasive species, implying this explains knotgrass invasion in warming Alaska. Text 2 directly tests this in Alaska and finds that despite significant temperature variation year-to-year, both species showed no differential growth.

steps.title: Match the contrast to the answer
steps.content:
The fact that temperature variation didn't produce differential growth undermines the assumption that non-boreal warming patterns apply to the Alaskan boreal context—a direct challenge to generalizability.

  • A claims Text 2 provides an alternative explanation (it doesn't)
  • B frames this as a methodology issue (it's a finding)
  • D says the study invalidates Text 1's research (too extreme—the problem is whether it applies elsewhere
, not whether it's valid where it came from).

That matches choice C — C is correct.
